title: quercus × runcinata
intro: Quercus × runcinata is a hybrid oak plant resulting from the cross between Quercus robur and Quercus petraea, characterized by its unique leaf morphology and distribution of acorns. The leaves of Quercus × runcinata have been reported to possess medicinal properties, particularly in their astringent and antiseptic qualities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Quercus × runcinata leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and applications.
